title: BWC & Incident Records: Roland Caballero Shootout — Three HPD Officers Shot, Vehicle Pursuit & SWAT Standoff – Houston, TX (January 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the shooting of three Houston Police Department officers by suspect Roland Caballero, the subsequent vehicle pursuit, carjacking, and SWAT standoff.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: January 27, 2022
Location: Initial shooting at McGowen Street and Hutchins Street (Third Ward area); SWAT standoff at 1800 block of Lockwood Drive (Fifth Ward), Houston, Texas
Suspect Name: Roland Caballero, 31, of Houston
Involved Officers: Officers Hayden, Gadson, Alvarez, Ricinos-Diaz, and additional SWAT officers
 
Incident Summary: Officers responded to a threat-to-life call and pursued Caballero's Dodge Charger. After crashing near McGowen and Hutchins streets, Caballero exited and opened fire with a fully automatic weapon, striking three officers. He then carjacked a white Mercedes and fled. Officers later located the vehicle at his Lockwood Drive residence, where a SWAT standoff ensued with additional shots fired. Caballero surrendered after approximately five hours. He was charged with three counts of attempted capital murder of a police officer, aggravated robbery, and federal weapons charges.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Officers Hayden, Gadson, Alvarez, Ricinos-Diaz, and all other HPD officers who were involved in the initial traffic stop, vehicle pursuit, and shootout near McGowen and Hutchins streets.
 
All BWC footage from SWAT officers who responded to and engaged the suspect during the standoff at his Lockwood Drive residence.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) footage from all HPD patrol vehicles involved in the pursuit and present at both the initial shooting scene and the standoff location.
 
Any surveillance camera footage recovered by investigators from the area of the initial crash and shootout.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives, supplemental reports, and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the initial threat-to-life call, the pursuit, the officer-down calls, and the SWAT standoff.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings following Caballero's surrender and transport to the hospital.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Juan Ayon-Barraza Kidnapping Pursuit & Fatal Officer-Involved Shooting – Richmond, CA (April 2020)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal officer-involved shooting of kidnapping suspect Juan Ayon-Barraza following a high-speed multi-agency pursuit that ended in Richmond.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: April 16, 2020
Location: South 37th Street and Stege Avenue / Carlson Boulevard, Richmond, California
Suspect Name: Juan Carlos Ayon-Barraza, 24, of Vacaville/Vallejo
Victim: Tanisha Thomas, 29, of Vallejo (kidnapping victim)
Involved Officers: Richmond PD Officer Deorian; Oakland PD Officers Recendez and Hohn
Agencies Involved: Richmond Police Department, Oakland Police Department, Moraga Police Department, Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office, California Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: On the morning of April 16, 2020, Vallejo Police received a missing person report for 29-year-old Tanisha Thomas, who was last seen with Ayon-Barraza. That evening at approximately 6:45 p.m., a cyclist called 911 reporting a man in a white Chevrolet Astro van dumping what appeared to be a body on the side of the road near Pinehurst Road and Skyline Boulevard in unincorporated Contra Costa County. Responding officers from Oakland, Moraga, and the Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office located the victim, who had been shot in the head and severely beaten. The suspect's van was spotted and a pursuit ensued, reaching speeds over 100 mph on Interstate 580 and Highway 13. The 20-minute chase ended in Richmond at South 37th Street and Stege Avenue, where Ayon-Barraza made a U-turn and rammed his van into Oakland police vehicles, injuring two officers. Officers from Richmond and Oakland fired on the suspect after he pointed a firearm in their direction. Ayon-Barraza sustained a fatal gunshot wound and was pronounced dead at the scene. Thomas died approximately one month later from her injuries.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Richmond Police Officer Deorian and any other Richmond PD officers who responded to or were involved in the pursuit and shooting.
 
All BWC footage from Oakland Police Officers Recendez and Hohn and any other Oakland PD officers present during the pursuit and final confrontation.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) footage from all Richmond PD and Oakland PD patrol vehicles involved in the pursuit and at the shooting scene.
 
All helicopter or aerial camera footage captured during the pursuit.
 
The complete incident report from the Richmond Police Department, including all officer narratives and supplemental reports.
 
All 911 call recordings related to this incident, including the initial call from the cyclist reporting the body being dumped and any calls from the pursuit and shooting scene.
 
All dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the pursuit and officer-involved shooting.
 
Any use-of-force reports filed by officers who discharged their weapons.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Stephanie Bloodworth DUI Pursuit – Duck Key, FL (February 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of Stephanie Marie Bloodworth following a vehicle pursuit on Duck Key.

Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: February 21, 2022
Location: North Bahama Drive, Duck Key, Monroe County, Florida
Suspect Name: Stephanie Marie Bloodworth (DOB: 11/01/1997)
Arresting Agency: Monroe County Sheriff's Office
Arresting Deputy: Deputy Connor O'Neill

Incident Summary: Multiple deputies responded to Duck Key at approximately 6:30 p.m. after a caller reported a gray Tesla nearly hit a tree and pedestrians. Deputy Connor O'Neill spotted the Tesla parked on North Bahama Drive and the driver matched the caller's description. The driver, later identified as Bloodworth, put the car in drive and sped away as Deputy O'Neill approached. A vehicle pursuit ensued through Duck Key streets with Bloodworth running stop signs at a high rate of speed. Bloodworth stopped briefly, pushed Deputy O'Neill away, and fled a second time before being stopped and detained. She resisted arrest, kicked multiple deputies, and was ultimately taken into custody. Breathalyzer results showed a blood alcohol level of .251, over three times the legal limit. She was charged with fleeing and eluding, DUI, resisting arrest, and reckless driving.

Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Deputy Connor O'Neill and all other responding deputies involved in the initial contact, vehicle pursuit, arrest, and transport of Stephanie Bloodworth.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) and in-car camera footage from all responding patrol vehicles present during the pursuit, arrest, and transport.
 
Any rear-seat camera footage from the patrol vehicle in which Bloodworth was transported.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives and supplemental reports.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the initial reckless driving report and subsequent pursuit.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings, including sobriety evaluation footage from the Monroe County Detention Center.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

title: Obama Presidential Library: Records Regarding the Death of Eduard Shevardnadze (July 2014)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Records Regarding the Death of Eduard Shevardnadze (July 2014)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) and the Presidential Records Act (44 U.S.C. §§ 2201–2207), I hereby request access to and copies of records maintained by the Barack Obama Presidential Library relating to the death and state funeral of former Georgian President and Soviet Foreign Minister Eduard Shevardnadze, who died on July 7, 2014.

Request Scope
I am seeking all records created or received between July 1, 2014, and July 31, 2014, pertaining to the U.S. government's response to Shevardnadze's death and funeral. This request includes, but is not limited to:

Drafting History: All drafts, revisions, and internal memoranda related to the preparation of any official White House statement on the death of Eduard Shevardnadze, including communications originating from or passing through the Office of the Press Secretary and the National Security Council (NSC) Executive Secretariat.

Official Correspondence: Final versions of any formal letters of condolence or diplomatic cables (including those transmitted via the State Department) from President Barack Obama or Vice President Joseph Biden addressed to the Shevardnadze family, President Giorgi Margvelashvili, Ambassador Archil Gegeshidze, Foreign Minister Maia Panjikidze or Prime Minister Irakli Garibashvili of Georgia.

Briefing Materials: Any NSC "Point Papers," decision memos, or briefing memoranda prepared for the President or Vice President concerning Eduard Shevardnadze's historical legacy, the significance of his funeral, and the diplomatic implications for U.S.-Georgian and U.S.-Russian relations.

Technical Specifications
To facilitate a thorough search, I request that the library examine the following record systems:

WHORM (White House Office of Records Management) Subject Files under code CO054 (Georgia) and FO (Foreign Affairs) for the specified date range;
NSC Subject Files and Country Files categorized under Georgia or the former Soviet Union/Russia;
Any applicable ME (Messages) subject files relating to condolences transmitted during this period;
The Alpha Files of the WHORM system for correspondence addressed to or received from Georgian government officials.

If digital records are identified, please provide associated metadata, including timestamps and sender/recipient fields.

Suggested Search Terms and Keywords
To assist archival staff in conducting electronic and full-text searches of relevant databases, I suggest the following keywords and phrases be applied across all applicable record systems:

Eduard Shevardnadze (including variant spellings: Shevardnadze, Ševardnadze)
Georgia / Georgian government / Republic of Georgia
Condolence / condolences / letter of condolence / message of sympathy
Giorgi Margvelashvili (President of Georgia)
Archil Gegeshidze (Georgian Ambassador to the United States)
Maia Panjikidze (Georgian Foreign Minister)
Irakli Garibashvili (Prime Minister of Georgia)
Soviet Foreign Minister / Cold War / glasnost / perestroika
Tbilisi / Gori (Shevardnadze's funeral was held in Tbilisi)
U.S.-Georgia relations / U.S.-Georgian relations
NSC / National Security Council / Senior Director for Russia and Eurasia
State Department / cable / diplomatic cable / démarche
July 2014 / funeral / state funeral

I am requesting these records in my capacity as an independent researcher focused on diplomatic history and U.S.-Georgian relations. Disclosure of these records is in the public interest because they document how the United States government managed the legacy of a pivotal Cold War figure during a period of acute geopolitical sensitivity — namely, the aftermath of Russia's annexation of Crimea in March 2014. This information is not primarily in my commercial interest. Accordingly, I request a waiver of all processing fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: ATF — Weapons Trace Records and Investigative Files — North Hollywood Shootout — February 28, 1997

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ATF — Weapons Trace Records and Investigative Files — North Hollywood Shootout — February 28, 1997
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing to request records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552.
I request all records of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives relating to:
1. All weapons trace requests submitted in connection with the bank robbery and officer-involved shooting that occurred on February 28, 1997, at Bank of America Branch 384, 6600 Laurel Canyon Boulevard, North Hollywood, California. This includes eTrace records, Firearms Tracing System (FTS) records, and any correspondence with the Los Angeles Police Department or any other law enforcement agency arising from trace requests related to this incident.
2. All ATF records relating to Larry Eugene Phillips Jr., date of birth September 20, 1970, also known as Larry Eugene Warfel, who was killed at the above location on February 28, 1997.
3. All records relating to Emil Decebal Matasareanu, date of birth July 19, 1968, who was killed at the above location on February 28, 1997.
4. Any ATF investigative report, field report, or correspondence relating to the modification or conversion of the firearms used in the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood incident, including but not limited to any assessment of whether those firearms were illegally converted to fire automatically or near-automatically.
5. Any correspondence between ATF and the Los Angeles Police Department, the Los Angeles District Attorney's Office, or the Federal Bureau of Investigation relating to the firearms, suspects, or investigation described above.
Both subjects named above are deceased. Both died on February 28, 1997. Privacy Act and FOIA personal privacy exemptions are therefore significantly reduced for these records.
I request that any reasonably segregable portion of any responsive record be provided even if other portions are withheld. Please identify the specific exemption claimed for any withheld material.
Fee Waiver Request
I request a full waiver of search, duplication, and review fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). I am a journalist and author researching a book on the 1997 North Hollywood shootout for public interest publication. Disclosure of these records is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of ATF's role in the investigation of the weapons used in one of the most significant armed confrontations in American law enforcement history. The requested records are not sought for commercial use.
If a fee waiver is not granted, I request to be notified before any fees exceeding $25.00 are incurred, and I agree to pay reasonable fees up to that amount without prior notification.
Format
I request records in electronic format where available. If records exist only in paper form, I request copies.
If any portion of this request is denied, I request an itemized index of withheld material pursuant to Vaughn v. Rosen, 484 F.2d 820 (D.C. Cir. 1973).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current status of any referral received by USDA OIG regarding SNAP/FoodShare fraud by Tanya Marie Anderson (DOB 10/15/1983), Dane County, Wisconsin.

2. Any investigation opened by USDA OIG based on the Intentional Program Violation finding in Wisconsin DHS Administrative Hearing Case No. FOF-215399, issued December 20, 2024, finding $52,917 in SNAP/FoodShare overpayments.

3. Any communications between USDA OIG and the Wisconsin DHS Office of Inspector General regarding Tanya Marie Anderson's benefit fraud, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

FEE WAIVER: I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). Disclosure is in the public interest — it concerns USDA OIG oversight of SNAP fraud following a documented IPV finding. Not for commercial purpose.


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All quality assurance review documents for Dane County CPS investigations from January 1, 2019 through the present. Dane County DCDHS has represented that it has conducted ZERO internal quality assurance reviews during this period. I am requesting state-level records to verify whether DCF has conducted its own reviews of Dane County's CPS operations.

2. All state-level oversight reports, audits, program reviews, or compliance assessments of Dane County DCDHS child protective services from January 1, 2019 through the present, including any CFSRs, PIPs, or other federal/state review documents.

3. Any state-level complaints received regarding CPS workers Heather Stertz or Angela Ivan (Dane County DCDHS) from January 1, 2023 through the present, including complaints filed with the DCF Office of the Secretary, DHS OIG, or any other state-level intake mechanism.

This request supplements my January 13, 2026 request, receipt confirmed, to which I have not yet received a substantive response.

SCOPE: I am requesting institutional oversight records, not individual case records protected under Wis. Stat. 48.78. To the extent any responsive records contain individually identifying information, I request redaction and production of the remainder.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:


1. All internal emails and communications mentioning "Lennon" or "Liam Lennon" between January 1, 2022 and the present, among WCSD staff, including but not limited to: Olivia Swanton, Heather Hammer (also known as Heather Hamer), Tiffany Loken, Monica Kelsey-Brown, Tim Schell, and AP Zibell.

2. Officer Mitch Flora's School Resource Officer (SRO) employment contract or agreement with WCSD and the Village of Waunakee, including the complete job description, dual-role responsibilities, and reporting chain of command.

3. All CPS reports filed by WCSD staff regarding the Lennon family from January 1, 2022 through June 30, 2023, including the identity of the reporter and the content of each report.

4. Liam Lennon's complete pupil records under Wis. Stat. section 118.125, including all discipline records, incident reports, behavioral referrals, bullying complaints (filed by or against Liam), and all 504 plan documentation.

This request supersedes and consolidates my prior open records requests to the extent they overlap.

AUTHORITY: Items 1-3 are requested under Wis. Stat. sections 19.31-19.39. Item 4 is a parental records request under Wis. Stat. section 118.125(2) and FERPA, 20 U.S.C. section 1232g.



In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RESUBMISSION — FOUR PRIOR REQUESTS UNANSWERED (740+ DAYS)

NOTICE: I submitted four (4) open records requests to the Waunakee Police Department in March 2024. As of this date — over 740 days later — I have received ZERO responses to any of those requests. This resubmission consolidates those requests and serves as formal notice that I will seek mandamus relief under Wis. Stat. section 19.37(1) if no response is received within a reasonable time.

Pursuant to Wisconsin's Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. sections 19.31 through 19.39, I hereby request copies of the following records:

1. The complete body camera policy for the Waunakee Police Department in effect from January 1, 2023 through June 30, 2023, including any exceptions, opt-out provisions, or discretionary guidelines for when body cameras are not required.

2. The complete Memorandum of Understanding or Intergovernmental Agreement between the Village of Waunakee / Waunakee Police Department and the Waunakee Community School District regarding the School Resource Officer (SRO) program, including all versions in effect from January 1, 2020 through the present.

3. All records relating to the citizen complaint filed by Alexander Lennon against Officer Mitch Flora on February 28, 2024, including any investigation, findings, disciplinary action, or disposition.

4. All records of calls for service involving the address 5876 Woodland Drive, Waunakee, WI, from January 1, 2019 through the January 1, 2024.

5. All records relating to WPD Case No. 25-124662 (Detective Jason Carbon investigation of Tanya Marie Anderson), including all supplements, correspondence with the Dane County District Attorney's Office, and the final disposition.

MANDAMUS NOTICE: Under Wis. Stat. section 19.37(1), if an authority withholds a record or part of a record, the requester may bring an action for mandamus. The court shall award reasonable attorney fees, damages, and other actual costs to the requester if the requester prevails in whole or in substantial part.

I request a fee waiver based on the public interest. If fees apply and exceed $25.00, please notify me before proceeding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: TUHSD Race/Ethnicity Data
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide any and all of the the following  that pertain to  the 2021-22, 2022-23, 2023-24, 2024-25, and/or 2025-26 academic years—

                    1.	Any and all records reflecting data bearing on students’ social, emotional, or psychological well-being, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	Youth Truth Survey results;
                                    b.	California Healthy Kids Survey results (CHKS); and
                                     c.	 Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific surveys or measures.

                    2.	Any and all records reflecting annual and/or semi-annual academic data, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	DFI Semester Grades;
                                    b.	Local Control and Accountability Plans (LCAP);
                                     c.	School Accountability Report Cards (SARC); and
                                    d.	Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific data that is or can be disaggregated by race.

                    3.	Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of the District pertaining to any of the aforementioned data.

Where school-specific data is disaggregated by grade, please provide combined data for all grades as well where available; our goal with this request is to ensure that racial/ethnic groups whose numbers are too small to be reportable at the grade level will reach reportable numbers when all grade levels are included in the data for a given school year.

Please provide any raw data in excel or csv format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request for public records.

Please provide all records, in any format (including audio, video, written, electronic, and digital records) relating to 911 calls, non-emergency calls, dispatch communications, or other police activity involving phone number 720-799-5294 and/or the address 2362 Shenandoah Rd, Palm Bay, FL 32905, occurring between September 9, 2023 and September 14, 2023.

This request includes records received, logged, transferred, handled, generated, or referenced by your agency, regardless of whether a police response ultimately occurred.

Specifically, please include:

1. Phone Call Recordings

Audio recordings of all 911 calls placed from 720-799-5294 during the specified time period

Audio recordings of non-emergency calls placed to or received by your agency from 720-799-5294

Audio recordings of any transferred calls or call-backs between dispatch, officers, or other agencies

2. Call Logs & Metadata

Call logs showing the date, time, and duration of each call

Call-taker or dispatcher notes

Call classification and disposition codes

Incident, event, or case numbers associated with each call

3. Dispatch & CAD Records

All CAD entries and calls for service associated with phone number 720-799-5294

All CAD entries and calls for service associated with the address 2362 Shenandoah Rd, Palm Bay, FL 32905 during the requested period

Dispatch notes and time-stamped activity logs

Unit assignments, dispatch decisions, and response actions

4. Radio & Dispatch Communications

Audio recordings of dispatch radio traffic and unit-to-unit radio communications related to these calls or the address

5. Body Camera & Dash Camera Footage

All body-worn camera footage and dash camera footage associated with CAD calls or calls for service involving the phone number or address during the specified time period

6. Related Reports

Any incident reports, offense reports, or supplemental reports generated as a result of these calls or calls for service

Please include all responsive records regardless of whether an officer was dispatched, an arrest occurred, or a report was finalized.

If any records are withheld or redacted, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon and provide the non-exempt portions.

If any responsive records have been destroyed, lost, or no longer exist, please provide documentation reflecting that fact.

Please provide records in electronic format where available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Ashley Kautz
